message:
fix(fp-index): route align_union through align_non_unique when either side has duplicates

`align_union` was unconditionally using the fast-path that builds
`position_map_first_ref()` on both inputs. That helper assumes each label
is unique — it only records the *first* position of any label — so when
either the left or the right index contains duplicate labels, the
resulting plan silently dropped the second-and-later occurrences and
produced an incorrect outer join (missing rows, wrong row counts).

The `align_non_unique` helper already exists in this file (defined at
line 749) and handles Inner/Left/Right/Outer duplicate-aware plans via
explicit label-bucket iteration. Added a short-circuit at the top of
`align_union`: when `left.has_duplicates() || right.has_duplicates()`,
delegate to `align_non_unique(left, right, AlignMode::Outer)` and return
the plan it produces. The uniqueness fast-path is still used when both
inputs are unique, so the common case is unchanged.

Mirrors the duplicate-handling already present in the Inner/Left/Right
cousins of this function.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
